cluster_name: Pope Leo XIV issues 'Magnifica Humanitas' encyclical on AI governance
tldr: Headlines worldwide collapse the 200-page encyclical into a single regulatory demand — to ‘disarm’ and regulate AI — with French outlets repeating the ‘désarmer’ phrase verbatim across five distinct mastheads. A secondary headline current, led by The Hindu Intl and Middle East Eye, foregrounds the encyclical’s pointed rejection of Trump-backed ‘just war’ doctrine ahead of the AI governance message. El Pais deploys ‘tecnofascismo’ in its headline — a term appearing in no other outlet in the corpus — escalating the power-concentration frame into explicitly anti-fascist register. Mother Jones alone frames the Pope’s stance as political ‘resistance,’ while Politico Europe adds a meta-angle on Big Tech lobbying the Vatican, both diverging from the dominant regulatory framing.

frames.sub_frame: Disarm and regulate AI: papal call to action

evidence.quote: Pope Leo urges world to 'slow down' on AI
AI must serve humanity not concentrate power
Papa Leão XIV alerta para riscos da Inteligência Artificial

frames.sub_frame: AI vs. human dignity: moral imperative

evidence.quote: Quien controla la IA impondrá su visión moral
Remaining human in the age of algorithms

frames.sub_frame: AI warfare and 'just war' doctrine rejected

evidence.quote: Pope calls US-backed ‘just war’ idea outdated
Pope Leo calls on Catholics to 'move beyond' just war
advertencias sobre el poder, la IA y las guerras
le monde de la tech a tenté d’influencer la position du pape

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only headline in the corpus to deploy 'tecnofascismo', framing AI power concentration as a fascist threat — a register absent from all other outlets including other left-leaning mastheads.
Frames the Pope's encyclical as political 'resistance' ('Is Pope Leo Joining the Anti-AI Resistance?'), uniquely casting the story as a partisan alignment rather than a governance or moral event.
Frames the encyclical as a one-year pontificate milestone ('cartão de visitas'), foregrounding the anniversary angle absent from all other corpus headlines.

bottom_line: The headline layer presents Pope Leo XIV’s encyclical as a single regulatory demand — stop arming AI and regulate it now — with ‘disarm’ and ‘slow down’ the near-universal register across wires, European broadsheets, and Anglophone regional outlets. The ‘just war’ rebuttal surfaces as a concentrated secondary frame in South Asian and Middle Eastern headlines. Only outliers — El Pais’s ‘tecnofascismo,’ Mother Jones’s ‘resistance,’ Politico’s tech-lobbying meta-angle — break from this dominant disarm-AI headline consensus.
